title: Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis
intro: Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis, also known as Hoabinhian tabernaemontana, is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the Apocynaceae family, native to Indochina. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various diseases and infections. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis include alkaloids, particularly voacangine and voacristine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family consists of over 5,000 species of flowering plants, including various species of vines, shrubs, and trees. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The species hoabinhensis is a type of flowering plant that is specific to certain regions. Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis is also known as the Hoabinh Tabernaemontana.
uses: Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis is a plant species native to Indochina, and its roots are used in traditional medicine. The roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. They are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant is also used to treat skin problems and wounds due to its antibacterial properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Tabernaemontana hoabinhensis.
